englishDefinitions: (intransitive, copulative) To communicate with a base etc, by telephone.
(transitive) To report; communicate (a message) by telephone or similar.
(transitive) To summon someone, especially for help or advice.
(transitive) To withdraw something from sale or circulation.
(transitive) To request immediate repayment of (a debt).
persianTranslation: (ناگذر، زوجی) برای برقراری ارتباط با پایگاه و غیره، از طریق تلفن.
(متعدی) گزارش کردن. برقراری ارتباط (پیام) از طریق تلفن یا موارد مشابه.
(متعدی) احضار کسی مخصوصاً برای کمک یا نصیحت.
(متعدی) بیرون کشیدن چیزی از فروش یا گردش.
examples: I was too unwell to work yesterday so I called in sick.
The hoaxer called in a bomb threat.
I'll call in this afternoon to pick up my prescription.
The government called in the army to deal with the riots.
